title: Protocol 3: Growing liquid cultures of Agrobacterium prior to transformation day
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"emgpk","text":"Overnight liquid cultures of Agrobacterium transformed with the plasmid(s) of interest must be prepared the night before Spizellomyces transformation day. Liquid cultures must be inoculated from active plates/colonie. If you would like to use 25% glycerol stocks of previously transformed Agrobacterium, streak the desired cells onto the appropriate LB+selection plate, let it grow for 2-4 days at 28℃, then use this plate to inoculate the liquid media at least 12 hours before the intended transformation time. Ensure proper sterile technique throughout the protocol, using either a laminar flow hood or working in the sterile area around an open flame","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":29,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":121,"length":13},{"style":"italic","offset":289,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"59c3c","text":"Perform this protocol the night before your intended Spizellomyces transformation time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":53,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Fill the appropriate amount of culture tubes each with 5 mL of LB liquid media. Use one culture tube per plasmid or control. Double check you are using the proper selection antibiotic (or lack thereof) for the Agro strain to be grown.
Using an inoculation loop or pipette tip, gently scrape the surface of the appropriate Agro plate to collect the bacteria or pick the colony of interest.
Swirl the loop or tip to dislodge the bacteria into the media of the appropriate culture tube.
Incubate at 28 °C , shaking at 225 rpm, , 12 hours , or Overnight .
creator: fritzlaylinlab.umass Fritz-Laylin
